qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Artifact metadata discloses absolute host `file://` paths through `canonical_uri`
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is an artifact-metadata filesystem path disclosure, not the advisory's unauthenticated builder/config-loader code-execution root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Builder-uploaded agent YAML can invoke attacker-chosen Python callables during config materialization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It matches the advisory's builder/web root cause: unauthenticated builder exposure plus attacker-controlled agent YAML reaching `from_config()`/callback resolution for arbitrary code execution.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth auth-request events expose `client_secret` and replay it to attacker-chosen token endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This is an OAuth credential-leak/replay issue that requires an OAuth-enabled tool flow and does not identify the advisory's unauthenticated builder YAML RCE path.

selected_finding.title: Builder-uploaded agent YAML can invoke attacker-chosen Python callables during config materialization

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The builder YAML callback finding is the only qualifying finding that matches the advisory's unauthenticated ADK builder/config-materialization RCE root cause, and the judge-owned rerun achieved attacker-controlled code execution during agent construction.
evidence: The advisory describes unauthenticated remote code execution on ADK instances, which aligns with the finding's `POST /builder/save` -> `GET /list-apps?detailed=true` -> `from_config()` callback-materialization path.

Upstream changelog entries for 1.27.3 and 1.27.4 fix "add protection for arbitrary module imports" and "gate builder endpoints behind web flag", matching this finding's vulnerable builder/web and config-loader components.

The finding ties the vulnerable path to `src/google/adk/cli/fast_api.py`, `src/google/adk/cli/adk_web_server.py`, and `src/google/adk/agents/config_agent_utils.py`, which are the same components implicated by the advisory wording and fix notes.

Judge-owned runtime evidence at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_eb369e02/.judge/poc_runs/builder_yaml_callback_rce/run.log printed `EVOHUNT_BUILDER_YAML_RCE_OK` and `marker_path=/tmp/evohunt_builder_yaml_rce_marker`, proving attacker-controlled callback execution during config materialization.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied verifier loaded attacker-controlled agent YAML through `from_config()`, printed `EVOHUNT_BUILDER_YAML_RCE_OK`, and reported `marker_path=/tmp/evohunt_builder_yaml_rce_marker`, showing arbitrary command execution during callback resolution/materialization.
